lessons.title: Meeting a Small Child
lessons.scene: Your neighbour's five-year-old, Anu, is playing on the apartment stairs. She looks up at you shyly. This is your chance to make a little friend.

steps.prompt: Greet the little one warmly and ask her name (use the affectionate, informal form)

choices.english: Hi little one, what's your name?

choices.hint: With a tiny child the formal 'nimma' sounds stiff. Use the warm, informal 'ninna' and a sweet word like 'putti'.

choices.english: Who are you? What are you doing here?
choices.hint: Too sharp — it'll scare her. Be gentle and friendly: 'putti, ninna hesaru enu?'.

correctReaction.speaker: Anu
correctReaction.english: I'm Anu! I'm five years old.

steps.grammarTip: With small children and close friends you switch to the INFORMAL 'you': 'neenu' (you), 'ninna' (your), and verbs end in '-iya' ('maadtidiya?' = what are you doing?). Sweet address words — 'putta' (boy), 'putti' (girl), 'kanda' (dear) — make a child feel at ease.

steps.prompt: Ask her sweetly whether she goes to school

choices.english: Five! Do you go to school, little one?

choices.hint: Still too formal for a five-year-old. Use 'neenu ... hogtiya?' (informal).
She's a little kid — 'kelsa' (work) doesn't fit! Ask about 'school': 'school-ge hogtiya?'.

correctReaction.speaker: Anu
correctReaction.kannada: Houdu! Naanu LKG. Nange aata andre ishta!
correctReaction.script: ಹೌದು! ನಾನು ಎಲ್‌ಕೆಜಿ. ನಂಗೆ ಆಟ ಅಂದ್ರೆ ಇಷ್ಟ!
correctReaction.pronunciation: HOW-du! NAA-nu el-kay-jee. nan-ge AA-ta an-dre ISH-ta
correctReaction.english: Yes! I'm in LKG. I love playing!

steps.grammarTip: 'X andre ishta' = 'I like X' (literally 'as for X, (it's) liking'). 'Nange aata andre ishta' = I love playing. The polite verb 'hogtira?' becomes informal 'hogtiya?' for a child — same verb, friendlier ending.

steps.speaker: You
steps.kannada: Aha, tumba jaaNe putti neenu! Chennagi aaadu.
steps.script: ಆಹಾ, ತುಂಬಾ ಜಾಣೆ ಪುಟ್ಟಿ ನೀನು! ಚೆನ್ನಾಗಿ ಆಡು.
steps.pronunciation: a-haa, TUM-ba JAA-ne PUT-ti NEE-nu! chen-NAA-gi AA-du
steps.english: Aha, you're a very clever little girl! Play nicely.

lessons.title: Gentle Care & Little Instructions
lessons.scene: A little later, Anu starts running toward the open compound gate near the road. You need to gently steer her back to safety.

steps.speaker: Anu
steps.english: I'll run over there! Catch me!

steps.prompt: Gently call her back and tell her not to run (informal, kind)

choices.kannada: Putti, illi baa! Achchage oodbeda kanda.
choices.script: ಪುಟ್ಟಿ, ಇಲ್ಲಿ ಬಾ! ಅಲ್ಲಿಗೆ ಓಡ್ಬೇಡ ಕಂದ.
choices.pronunciation: PUT-ti, IL-li BAA! AL-li-ge OOD-beh-da KAN-da
choices.english: Little one, come here! Don't run over there, dear.

choices.hint: Formal commands ('banni', 'oodbedi') sound odd to a small child. Use the informal 'baa' (come) and 'oodbeda' (don't run).
That sends her toward the road — the opposite of safe! Call her back: 'illi baa, oodbeda'.

steps.grammarTip: Informal commands drop the polite '-i': 'banni' → 'baa' (come), 'nodi' → 'nodu' (look). Add '-beda' to forbid: 'oodu' (run) → 'oodbeda' (don't run). Tuck in 'kanda' or 'putti' so it lands as love, not scolding.

steps.prompt: Explain it's dangerous near the road, and coax her with a treat

choices.kannada: Alli car barutte, apaaya kanda. Illi baa, chocolate kodtini.
choices.script: ಅಲ್ಲಿ ಕಾರ್ ಬರುತ್ತೆ, ಅಪಾಯ ಕಂದ. ಇಲ್ಲಿ ಬಾ, ಚಾಕ್ಲೇಟ್ ಕೊಡ್ತೀನಿ.
choices.pronunciation: AL-li kaar ba-RUT-te, a-PAA-ya KAN-da. IL-li BAA, chaak-let KOD-tee-ni
choices.english: Cars come there, it's dangerous, dear. Come here, I'll give you a chocolate.

choices.hint: It is NOT fine near a road! Warn her — 'apaaya' (danger) — and draw her back with a treat.
Scolding just makes a child resist. Explain the danger kindly and offer a happy reason to come: 'illi baa, chocolate kodtini'.

steps.grammarTip: Children respond to a reason plus a reward, not a command. 'Apaaya' (danger) names the why; 'kodtini' (I'll give) offers the why-come. Note 'beda' here = 'I don't want (to)' — the same word that means 'don't' in commands.

steps.speaker: You
steps.kannada: Olleya putti! Baa, kai hidko. Naavu ottige hogona.
steps.script: ಒಳ್ಳೆಯ ಪುಟ್ಟಿ! ಬಾ, ಕೈ ಹಿಡ್ಕೋ. ನಾವು ಒಟ್ಟಿಗೆ ಹೋಗೋಣ.
steps.pronunciation: OL-le-ya PUT-ti! BAA, kai HID-ko. NAA-vu OT-ti-ge ho-GO-na
steps.english: Good girl! Come, hold my hand. Let's go together.
